claim: 30Y Treasury yield closes at or above 5.25% at least once before June 17, 2026.
falsification_criteria: The 30Y yield closes below 5.25% on every trading session through June 17.
grading_note: Fiscal premium re-pricing has momentum and no visible catalyst to arrest it; 10bps of additional upside from 5.147% is a conservative extrapolation of a move already in progress. The market has not priced a credible US fiscal consolidation, so the path of least resistance for the long end remains higher.

claim: SLV's total return from May 18 outperforms GLD's by at least 5 percentage points by June 15, 2026.
falsification_criteria: SLV's return from May 18 minus GLD's return from May 18 is below +5pp on June 15.
grading_note: Silver's -10.3% weekly collapse is a velocity outlier driven by the dollar surge; the implied gold/silver ratio is at a historically stretched level that has consistently mean-reverted within weeks once dollar momentum plateaus. Industrial demand underpinning silver is intact and not the source of the dislocation.

claim: XLK beats XLE on total return (both from May 18) by at least 2pp by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: XLE's total return from May 18 meets or exceeds XLK's total return minus 2pp on August 16.
grading_note: Energy's +23% 20-day supply-shock surge has already priced a significant disruption premium; absent oil continuing to rise from here, XLE faces mean-reversion headwinds. The AI capex cycle underpinning tech is structural, and quarterly pullbacks after large runs have consistently been absorbed within the same quarter.

claim: The 3M-10Y Treasury spread (now +105bps) widens to at least 135bps by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: The 3M-10Y spread fails to close at or above 135bps on any single day through August 16.
grading_note: Fiscal premium continues lifting the 10Y while the 3M is anchored by a Fed on hold; 30bps of additional steepening is a conservative ask given the structural drivers of the current re-steepening are unchanged. The market is not pricing a credible mechanism to halt the long-end drift.

claim: EEM underperforms SPY by at least 5 percentage points on total return (both from May 18) by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: EEM's total return from May 18 is within 5pp of SPY's total return on August 16.
grading_note: Oil-importing EM economies face a twin squeeze: DXY near 99 tightens external financing conditions while oil +23% in 20 days expands import bills simultaneously. Historical analogs of this combination — dollar surge plus energy supply shock — have reliably produced EM equity underperformance within one to two quarters.

claim: The Fed implements no rate cut through its November 2026 FOMC meeting, leaving the target range unchanged from May 2026.
falsification_criteria: The Fed announces a cut of at least 25bps at any FOMC meeting on or before November 2026.
grading_note: The oil shock keeps near-term inflation risk alive precisely when the curve has normalized, removing the inversion-as-recession-signal urgency for emergency action; the 30Y at 5.15% signals fiscal concern that a dovish pivot would only worsen. Consensus is pricing easing probability that current data does not yet support.

claim: DXY closes below 95.00 at least once before May 18, 2027.
falsification_criteria: DXY closes at or above 95.00 on every trading day through May 18, 2027.
grading_note: The week's DXY rally to 99 is a tactical bounce, not a structural reversal; US twin deficits, fiscal premium concerns that crowd out foreign capital, and eventual Fed easing create medium-term dollar headwinds that should reassert within twelve months. A 4% decline from current levels is a modest ask if the structural bears are right.

claim: The 30Y Treasury yield's calendar-year 2027 daily-close average exceeds 5.00%.
falsification_criteria: The arithmetic mean of all 30Y Treasury daily closes during calendar year 2027 is 5.00% or below.
grading_note: US deficit trajectory near 6-7% of GDP and a rising debt/GDP path have structurally re-established term premium in a way that requires either credible fiscal consolidation — politically improbable — or a recession severe enough to force emergency easing to reverse. Starting at 5.147% today, the 30Y would need a sustained rally of more than 15bps held across the entire year to disprove this.

claim: 30Y Treasury yield closes above 4.90% on every trading day through the June 20, 2026 FOMC meeting.
falsification_criteria: 30Y yield closes at or below 4.90% on any trading day at or before June 20, 2026.
grading_note: Term premium repricing is structural: the 3M anchor at 3.59% rules out a cyclical inflation driver, so the 30Y at 5.064% reflects fiscal risk, not a hot-economy overshoot. A 17bp+ decline within four weeks would require a macro shock that is not visible in either credit spreads or forward guidance.

claim: USO closes below 135.00 at least once before June 13, 2026, extending this week's reversal.
falsification_criteria: USO does not close below 135.00 at any point through June 13, 2026.
grading_note: The -4.93% weekly breakdown broke out of the prior consolidation range and reversed the 20-day tailwind; OPEC+ supply additions are ongoing and the dollar's stabilization near 99 removes a commodity support floor. Momentum signals are now bearish, and mean-reversion instincts toward prior highs are the consensus — which makes the continuation call the contrarian one.

claim: HYG closes below its May 22, 2026 closing level on June 19, 2026 — 4-week HY credit return turns negative.
falsification_criteria: HYG closes at or above its May 22, 2026 closing price on June 19, 2026.
grading_note: HYG and LQD both posted weekly gains while the 30Y closed at 5.064% — a divergence that credit veterans flag as a yellow warning, not a green light. Duration is the more structurally informed market; when the two must converge, the path of least resistance is credit widening rather than a 50bp+ yield compression inside a month.

claim: 30Y Treasury yield closes at or above 5.30% at least once before August 22, 2026.
falsification_criteria: 30Y yield does not close at or above 5.30% at any point through August 22, 2026.
grading_note: The 30Y already traded to 5.147% intraweek before this week's modest pullback to 5.064%; the summer Treasury issuance calendar is heavy and no credible fiscal consolidation path exists. This week's yield relief reflects a one-week pause in a repricing that has structural momentum, and 5.30% is only 24bp above last week's intraweek high.

claim: XLK total return from May 22 to August 22, 2026 is negative — tech gives back the 20-day parabola.
falsification_criteria: XLK closes above its May 22, 2026 closing price on August 22, 2026.
grading_note: A 12.59% 20-day run sets an extreme expectations bar requiring sustained megacap earnings beats every quarter. With the 30Y at 5.064% still a structural multiple-compression headwind, one disappointment in the AI capex narrative is sufficient to break the momentum; the consensus continuation view is precisely what makes the reversal call asymmetric.

claim: GLD closes above 430.00 at least once before August 22, 2026, as fiscal risk reignites the safe-haven bid.
falsification_criteria: GLD does not close above 430.00 at any point through August 22, 2026.
grading_note: Gold's -4.48% 20-day decline is a lag effect of elevated real rates — the same pattern that preceded prior gold recoveries once fiscal stress became the dominant narrative. The 30Y clearing 5% is exactly the kind of sustained signal that eventually shifts the market from 'real rate headwind' to 'fiscal hedge'; the silver recovery from its 20-day trough is a leading industrial/monetary indicator of the turn.

claim: XLE total return from May 22 to November 20, 2026 underperforms XLU total return over the same period.
falsification_criteria: XLE total return equals or exceeds XLU total return from May 22 to November 20, 2026.
grading_note: Energy's weekly momentum has cracked — -4.93% on USO against XLE's near-flat +0.08% signals leadership exhaustion, while Utilities flipped from the week's worst to best sector on yield relief. Structurally, elevated real rates cap cap-rate-sensitive assets but also reduce the demand outlook for energy; the defensive rotation has a longer runway than one week.

claim: 30Y Treasury yield does not close below 4.50% at any point through December 31, 2027.
falsification_criteria: 30Y yield closes below 4.50% at any point through December 31, 2027.
grading_note: The US fiscal trajectory shows no credible consolidation path: the era of Fed QE as a structural yield cap is over, foreign demand must be priced at incrementally higher yields to absorb continued issuance, and term premium has demonstrably returned. A sub-4.50% 30Y would require either a severe recession-driven demand collapse or a policy reversal that is not visible in any current data.
